output.code:
# Let's load the text file and inspect content and character positions.
path = '/mnt/data/file-1dQXGU7yytzYFNhCtTXe5M-text_to_analyse.txt'
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:400], text[-400:]

# Find the inner quoted hadith text
import re

# Let's find the line with the hadith
lines = text.splitlines()
lines[:5]

start_quote_idx = text.find('"', 0)  # first double quote in entire text
start_quote_idx, text[start_quote_idx:start_quote_idx+5]

# The start of hadith content is colon_quote_idx + 3
hadith_start = colon_quote_idx + 3
# The end is the next double quote after hadith_start
hadith_end = text.find('"', hadith_start)
(hadith_start, hadith_end, text[hadith_start:hadith_start+10], text[hadith_end-10:hadith_end])

q_phrase = "وَإِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ إِنِّي خَالِقٌ بَشَرًا مِنْ طِينٍ"
indices=[]
start=0
while True:
    idx = text.find(q_phrase, start)
    if idx == -1:
        break
    indices.append(idx)
    start = idx+1
indices, len(indices)

q_trunc = "وَإِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ إِنِّي خَ"
idx_trunc = text.find(q_trunc)
idx_trunc, text[idx_trunc:idx_trunc+len(q_trunc)] if idx_trunc!=-1 else None

last_newline = text.rfind('\n')
last_line = text[last_newline+1:]
last_line, last_newline

# compute start index of last line
start_last_line = last_newline + 1
start_last_line

output.code:
import json

# compute spans
spans = []

# hadith span
spans.append({
    "span_start": hadith_start,
    "span_end": hadith_end,
    "span_text": hadith_text,
    "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
    "is_valid": False,
    "aya_reference": {},
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": "خطأ",
    "completely_invalid": True
})

# quran corrected canonical text (assumed)
canonical_q = "إِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ إِنِّي خَالِقٌ بَشَرًا مِنْ طِينٍ"

# Add the ten full occurrences
for idx in indices:
    spans.append({
        "span_start": idx,
        "span_end": idx + len(q_phrase),
        "span_text": text[idx: idx + len(q_phrase)],
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "ص", "sura_number": 38, "aya_number": 71},
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": canonical_q,
        "completely_invalid": False
    })

# partial last one
spans.append({
    "span_start": start_last_line,
    "span_end": len(text),
    "span_text": last_line,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": False,
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "ص", "sura_number": 38, "aya_number": 71},
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": canonical_q,
    "completely_invalid": False
})

output = {"citations": spans}
print(json.dumps(output, ensure_ascii=False)[:500])

# Let's build the full output, and also verify the span_texts match expectations
from pprint import pprint

full_output = {"citations": spans}
# Verify first few
for i, c in enumerate(full_output["citations"]):
    print(i, c["span_start"], c["span_end"], c["citation_type"])
    # show text snippet for check
    print(text[c["span_start"]:c["span_end"]])
    print('---')
len(full_output["citations"])

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
